title: carpinus tschonoskii var. eximia
intro: Carpinus tschonoskii var. eximia, also known as the Chinese or Japanese hop hornbeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to East Asia. Traditionally, the plant's bark and leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Carpinus tschonoskii var. eximia include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as supporting respiratory and digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its bark and leaves, and can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or infusions.
